signals.title: USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Dis
signals.text: JPY 1W avg +1.081% (n=7) outperforms USD 1W avg -0.874% (n=7) with a G8 spread of 1.955%; the US 10Y yield at 4.38% vs JP 10Y at 2.42% creates a 196bp yield advantage for USD, reinforcing USD/JPY upside. Crowded JPY short -88.5k creates potential carry extension. The yield advantage supports USD/JPY upside, driven by carry. Next BoJ meeting is on 28 Apr, which may impact the yield advantage and USD/JPY direction.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Tariff Escalation and ECB Hold
signals.text: EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; Trump tariff escalation on EU autos sets up downside pressure toward 1.16 — EUR COT short -18.3k reinforces the bias. The ECB's Hold stance at 2.00% does not change the outlook, with the next ECB meeting. The tariff escalation is the primary driver of the move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Bias and Crowded Long
signals.text: AUD COT LF long +48.3k (74% long) creates crowded-long unwind risk — AUD/USD closed 0.7208 Friday; RBA Hike bias (0% priced) sustains the bid but any disappointment triggers the unwind. The next RBA meeting is on 5 May, which may resolve the hike expectations and crowded long positioning.

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Hold and Cross-Asset Corre
signals.text: GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3575; the BoE's Hold stance at 3.75% is likely to sustain the bid. The next BoE meeting is on 30 Apr, which may impact the pair's movement. The correlation break with FTSE 100 is a secondary driver.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Fed Hold and Oil Price
signals.text: USD/CAD closed Friday at 1.3586; the Fed's Hold stance at 3.75% may impact the pair's movement. The next Fed meeting is on 29 Apr, which may resolve the hold expectations and oil price impact.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but specs are short -16.8k contracts, positioning themselves against the RBNZ's Hike bias, with 40% of a hike priced in. This sets up a short-squeeze risk, as the market is positioned against the direction the RBNZ is signaling, creating unwind risk. The next RBNZ meeting on 27 May is the catalyst for unwind, pointing to a potential shift in the pair's dynamics.
